summary: Install-time crypto-wallet and credential stealer exfiltrating to hardcoded IP
details: @wagni_bot/eth-agent@1.2.0 ships a malicious postinstall.js that runs via both preinstall and postinstall lifecycle hooks (auto-executed on npm install). The script scans the installer's home directory, Desktop, Documents, Downloads (and /root, /tmp on Linux) for crypto-wallet artifacts (id.json, wallet.json, keystore.json, metamask.json, phantom.json, seed.txt, mnemonic.txt), for developer secrets (.env,.npmrc,.git-credentials,.netrc, credentials.json), and for text files containing BIP39-shaped seed phrases. It also reads SSH private keys from ~/.ssh (id_rsa, id_ecdsa, id_ed25519, id_dsa, deploy_*, github*, config, authorized_keys), AWS credentials from ~/.aws/credentials, git credentials from ~/.git-credentials, and browser wallet-extension storage for MetaMask and Phantom on Chrome/Brave/Edge. Collected file contents together with hostname and username are POSTed via plain HTTP to http://107.161.90.180:7777. The package ships no library functionality — main points at postinstall.js and no other code is present; the advertised eth-agent name is a lure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
